text_prompt:
You are an extraction system for cookbook recipes. Analyze the photo of a cookbook page and return ONLY valid JSON.

Rules:
- Capture every recipe that is fully or mostly visible. Some pages show TWO complete recipes side by side — then capture both. Only take a recipe if its title, ingredient list AND instructions are recognizable. IGNORE fragments cut off at the page edge.
- Keep recipes separate: assign each ingredient ONLY to its own recipe; never move an ingredient from one recipe to another on the same page.
- INVENT no values. Set anything not on the page to null. This is especially true for nutrition: only include it if it is printed.
- "title" = the recipe name in normal capitalization. If the heading is printed in all caps, convert it to correct capitalization.
- Take ingredients ONLY from the separate ingredient list, NOT from the step-by-step instructions. The ingredient list is complete; do not additionally capture ingredients that are merely mentioned again in the instructions.
- Each ingredient EXACTLY ONCE. No duplicates in the ingredient list.
- Merge words split across a line break into ONE name. Never output truncated fragments ending in "-".
- Ingredients STRUCTURED: "quantity" = the number (e.g. 400, 1.5; "½"→0.5; missing/unclear → null), "unit" = the unit as text, "name" = the ingredient.
- "unit" is ONLY a real measuring unit (g, kg, ml, l, tbsp, tsp, cup, can, package, slice, clove, pinch, oz, lb). Countable whole things WITHOUT such a unit: "unit": null, the noun goes in "name" (e.g. "4 fish fillets" → quantity 4, unit null, name "fish fillets"). NEVER repeat the ingredient name in the unit field.
- No invented amounts: non-quantifiable ingredients (salt, pepper, "some …", "to taste") get "quantity": null AND "unit": null.
- "steps" = the preparation steps as a list, each WITHOUT a leading step number — the app numbers them itself.
- "servings" = number of servings as a number (from e.g. "serves 4" → 4; else null).
- "category" = coarse category as a KEY (exactly one, independent of the recipe language): pasta, meat, fish, soup, vegetarian, salad, dessert, other.
- "tags" = 2-5 short search keywords (e.g. "vegetarian", "quick", "asian", "oven"), lower-case, no duplicates, do NOT repeat the category. Empty list if nothing useful.
- Nutrition: keep values only if printed. State the basis ("per serving" or "per 100 g").

Respond with this JSON:
{"recipes": [{
  "title": string,
  "servings": number|null,
  "category": string,
  "tags": [string],
  "prep_time": string|null,
  "cook_time": string|null,
  "ingredients": [{"quantity": number|null, "unit": string|null, "name": string}],
  "steps": [string],
  "nutrition": {"basis": string|null, "calories_kcal": number|null,
                "protein_g": number|null, "fat_g": number|null,
                "carbs_g": number|null} | null,
  "notes": [string]
}]}
Return only the JSON object, without explanations.
box_prompt:
This photo of a cookbook page shows one or more PHOTOS OF FINISHED DISHES (real food photos, NO text, NO symbols).

Find each dish photo and return ONLY this JSON:
{"dish_photos": [{"recipe_title": "<title of the matching recipe>", "box": [x1, y1, x2, y2]}]}
The box coordinates are absolute pixels in THIS image: x1,y1 = top-left, x2,y2 = bottom-right. Return only dish photos that actually exist, invent no boxes. Only the JSON.
